subject: eddy-lazzarin

sectors_excited.name: Zero-knowledge proofs / ZK infrastructure
sectors_excited.why: ZK is maturing from research into shipping infrastructure — zkEVMs and zk-rollups are moving toward production this year. It's the most important primitive for both scaling (succinct verification) and eventually privacy and identity. This is a multi-year infrastructure bet that compounds regardless of near-term price action.

sectors_excited.name: Account abstraction (ERC-4337) / smart-contract wallets
sectors_excited.why: Smart accounts unlock social recovery, gas sponsorship, session keys, and better default security — directly attacking the seed-phrase and self-custody UX problems. After a year of custodial failures, giving users safer self-custody without a 12-word panic is exactly the onboarding unlock the space needs. The 4337 spec gaining traction without a consensus change makes it deployable now.

sectors_excited.name: Ethereum L2 rollups / scaling
sectors_excited.why: The rollup-centric roadmap is the credible path to cheap blockspace. Lower fees are the precondition for applications that aren't just speculation — payments, identity, games, consumer. The builder activity on optimistic and ZK rollups is where real product is shipping through the bear market.

sectors_concerned.name: Centralized stablecoins' banking dependency
sectors_concerned.why: The USDC depeg during the SVB failure this month exposed how much 'on-chain' dollars still depend on a handful of traditional bank counterparties. Concentration in reserves plus fragile banking rails is a systemic risk the space underpriced. Decentralized and more diversified collateral models look comparatively more important after March.

sectors_concerned.name: US crypto banking rails / on-off ramps
sectors_concerned.why: With Silvergate winding down and Signature seized, the dollar plumbing connecting crypto to the banking system has been hit hard in a single month. Fewer banking partners means thinner, more fragile fiat ramps for US-based businesses — an operational risk independent of any protocol.

sectors_concerned.name: US regulatory environment / regulation-by-enforcement
sectors_concerned.why: Enforcement is escalating without clear rules — staking-as-a-service actions, the BUSD/Paxos move, and exchange-directed Wells notices create an environment where compliant builders can't get a clear answer. The near-term risk is that talent and companies move offshore rather than the technology being fixed.

sectors_concerned.name: Centralized exchanges / CeFi custody
sectors_concerned.why: Post-FTX, opaque centralized custody and commingling remain the dominant counterparty risk in the sector. 'Not your keys' is reinforced, and trust in unverifiable balance sheets should stay low until proof-of-reserves and real custody segregation become standard.

tokens_excited.name: ETH
tokens_excited.why: The upcoming Shanghai/Shapella upgrade enabling staked-ETH withdrawals removes a major overhang by completing the post-Merge staking lifecycle and de-risking staked positions. Combined with post-Merge issuance reduction and fee burn, the asset's monetary and staking dynamics look structurally stronger heading into Q2.

risk_regime.why: End-of-Q1 2023 is a split picture: this month's banking failures (Silvergate, SVB, Signature) and the USDC depeg exposed real fragility in crypto's fiat rails, while simultaneously strengthening the long-term case for credibly-neutral, self-custodial money — BTC and ETH rallied on banking stress. Intensifying US enforcement adds policy risk. As a long-horizon, builder-focused investor I stay constructive on the technology and keep deploying through the cycle, but near-term banking and regulatory uncertainty argue for a selective, neutral posture rather than aggressive risk-on.

notes: My loaded record for this date is empty — there are no prior verbatim statements available to me here — so I have no grounded or persisted citations to cite, and nothing is marked as such. Every item above is 'extrapolated': inferred from how an a16z-crypto builder reasons as of 2023-03-31, using only information available by today (FTX aftermath, the March failures of Silvergate/SVB/Signature, the USDC depeg and recovery, the upcoming Shanghai/Shapella upgrade, and escalating SEC enforcement). I left tokens_concerned empty rather than manufacture a specific call I can't honestly ground.
